county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1816; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Lewis County Clerk (Weston). Formed 1816 from Harrison. Weston State Hospital (Trans-Allegheny Lunatic Asylum) records may document families. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records. Lewis County Historical Society. Check Harrison for pre-1816 records. Oil and gas industry from the 1860s. Stonewall Jackson Lake area. FamilySearch has microfilmed records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Lincoln County Clerk (Hamlin). Formed 1867 (post-statehood) from Boone, Cabell, Kanawha, and Putnam. One of WV's younger counties. All pre-formation records in parent counties. Rural, mountainous county. Lincoln County Historical Society. Timber and coal industries in late 19th century. Good surviving records from formation.

county_metadata.record_losses: 1904 courthouse fire destroyed deed books and other early records
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1824; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Logan County Clerk (Logan). BURNED COUNTY -- 1904 courthouse fire destroyed deed books and other early records. Formed 1824 from Cabell, Kanawha, and Giles (VA). Major coal county -- Mine Wars history including Battle of Blair Mountain (1921), the largest armed labor uprising in US history. WVU and Marshall University have Mine Wars archives. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Check parent counties for earlier records. Substitute sources include state copies of land grants and census records. Hatfield family (of Hatfield-McCoy feud) records documented in county.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1842; became part of WV at statehood 1863
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Marion County Clerk (Fairmont). Formed 1842 from Harrison and Monongalia. Fairmont was a major coal and industrial center. Fairmont State University archives. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Good surviving records. Marion County Historical Society. Coal mining and glass industry important. Monongah mine disaster (1907, worst US mine disaster) records at WVU and NARA. Check parent counties for earlier records. Italian immigration to mining communities documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1835; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Northern Panhandle
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Marshall County Clerk (Moundsville). Formed 1835 from Ohio County. Moundsville is site of the Grave Creek Mound (Adena culture). West Virginia Penitentiary records (1876-1995) at WV Archives. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Marshall County Historical Society. Ohio River trade and industry records. Check Ohio County for pre-1835 records. Good surviving records. Glass and steel manufacturing.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1804; became part of WV at statehood 1863; Ohio River county
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Mason County Clerk (Point Pleasant). Formed 1804 from Kanawha. Point Pleasant was site of the Battle of Point Pleasant (1774, Lord Dunmore's War) -- considered by some as the first battle of the American Revolution. Tu-Endie-Wei State Park. Ohio River county with good surviving records. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Mason County Historical and Preservation Society. Enslaved population documented. Check Kanawha for pre-1804 records. TNT Area (WWII) records. Mothman legend area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1858; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and McDowell County Clerk (Welch). Formed 1858 from Tazewell (VA). Premier coal county -- once WV's most populous county (1950 census: 98,887). Coal company records essential -- company towns, company stores, and employee records. African American migration to coal fields documented in WVU archives and Eastern Regional Coal Archives (Bluefield). Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. McDowell County Historical Society. Gary, Keystone, Kimball, and other coal towns. Italian, Hungarian, and African American immigration well documented.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county; originally Virginia county formed 1837; became part of WV at statehood 1863; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Mercer County Clerk (Princeton). Formed 1837 from Giles (VA) and Tazewell (VA). Major coal county -- Pocahontas coalfield development from 1880s. Eastern Regional Coal Archives at Craft Memorial Library (Bluefield) are essential for coal family research. Pre-1863 records at LVA Richmond. Norfolk & Western Railway records. Mercer County Historical Society. Bluefield area industrial records. Civil War activity. Check parent Virginia counties for earlier records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA

county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Mineral County Clerk (Keyser). Formed 1866 (post-statehood) from Hampshire. All formation records in Hampshire County. B&O Railroad important -- Piedmont and Keyser were railroad division points. Potomac State College (WVU branch) area. Mineral County Historical Society. Fort Ashby (one of WV's oldest structures) area records. German settlement in North Branch Potomac Valley. Good surviving records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: West Virginia county formed 1895; coal mining region
county_metadata.research_tips: WV Archives (Charleston) and Mingo County Clerk (Williamson). Formed 1895 from Logan -- one of WV's youngest counties. Coal company town records essential. Matewan Massacre (1920) and Mine Wars records at WVU and Marshall. Hatfield-McCoy feud area -- extensive court records. Pre-formation records in Logan County. Mingo County Historical Society. Tug Fork communities. Railroad records (Norfolk & Western). Good surviving records from formation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: WV Archives microfilm and county clerk records; pre-1863 at LVA
